qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authentication bypass via Referer header leads to remote code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a Referer-header-based authentication bypass in the requireAuth middleware (CWE-287), where specific Referer patterns cause auth to be skipped entirely. The advisory describes a hardcoded fallback JWT signing secret 'frangoteam751' (CWE-321/CWE-798) that allows token forgery. These are fundamentally different root causes — one is a flawed middleware logic gate, the other is a hardcoded cryptographic key — even though both reside in server/api/jwt-helper.js and both result in authentication bypass.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a hardcoded fallback JWT signing secret ('frangoteam751') that enables token forgery (CWE-321/CWE-798). The only qualifying finding describes a Referer-header-based authentication bypass in the requireAuth middleware (CWE-287) that skips auth checks based on header patterns like '/fuxa' or 'localhost:'. These are distinct root causes: the advisory is about a static cryptographic key enabling JWT forgery, while the finding is about a middleware logic flaw that bypasses auth via HTTP header inspection. No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause.
evidence: Advisory: 'FUXA used a static fallback JWT signing secret (frangoteam751) when no secretCode was configured' — CWE-321, CWE-798
Finding: 'The requireAuth function allows requests to bypass authentication if the Referer header contains specific patterns' — CWE-287
Advisory root cause is a hardcoded cryptographic key; finding root cause is a flawed authentication middleware gate based on Referer header patterns
Both touch server/api/jwt-helper.js but describe different vulnerability mechanisms at different code locations within that file
